name: context-tools
description: Context management tools for Claude Code - provides intelligent codebase mapping with Python, Rust, and C++ parsing, duplicate detection, and MCP-powered symbol queries. Use this skill when working with large codebases that need automated indexing and context management.
capabilities: Parses Python, Rust, and C++ code to extract functions, classes, and methods
Generates searchable SQLite index for fast symbol lookups
Detects similar classes and functions that may be duplicates
Creates project manifest with build system and entry points
Stores learnings that persist across sessions
Provides MCP tools for instant symbol search (10-100x faster than grep)
author: ChipFlow
securitySummary: This is a legitimate open-source Claude Code plugin for codebase navigation. The static analyzer reported 606 potential issues with a NEEDS_AI recommendation, but all findings are false positives. Documentation examples using backticks and shell commands were incorrectly flagged as executable code. Common words like 'command' and 'control' were misidentified as C2 indicators. Standard development patterns (subprocess, sqlite3, file I/O) with hardcoded arguments were incorrectly flagged. The project is well-maintained with clear intent for codebase mapping and symbol search.
valueStatement: Claude struggles to understand large codebases without proper structure. This skill provides automatic project mapping, symbol indexing, and duplicate detection so Claude can find functions, classes, and files instantly without slow grep searches.
limitations: Only indexes Python, Rust, and C++ source files
Requires uv (Python package manager) to be installed
First-time indexing takes time on large codebases
Does not analyze non-code files like JSON, markdown, or configs

useCases.title: Explore complex codebases
useCases.description: Quickly find functions, classes, and methods by name pattern without slow grep searches across thousands of files

useCases.title: Map polyglot projects
useCases.description: Automatically detect build systems, entry points, and structure in Python/Rust/C++ projects

useCases.title: Find duplicate code
useCases.description: Identify similar classes and functions that may need consolidation or better documentation

promptTemplates.title: Find a function
promptTemplates.scenario: Search for functions matching a pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Use mcp__repo-map__search_symbols to find all functions matching the pattern 'setup_*' in this codebase

promptTemplates.title: List file contents
promptTemplates.scenario: See all symbols in a file
promptTemplates.prompt: Use mcp__repo-map__get_file_symbols to show me all classes and functions defined in src/utils.py

promptTemplates.title: Get function source
promptTemplates.scenario: Read full source of a symbol
promptTemplates.prompt: Use mcp__repo-map__get_symbol_content to get the complete source code for the 'User' class

promptTemplates.title: Find files by pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Use mcp__repo-map__list_files to find all Python files matching the pattern '**/models/*'

bestPractices: Run repo-map after adding new files to keep the index current
Record important discoveries in .claude/learnings.md before session ends
Use MCP tools instead of grep for finding functions and classes
Review similar class/function warnings to identify code duplication
antiPatterns: Using grep/find to search for function names when MCP tools are available
Ignoring the learnings reminder before context compaction
Skipping repo-map regeneration after major refactoring
Running MCP queries without checking if indexing is complete

faq.question: What languages does this support?
faq.answer: Python, Rust, and C++ (.cpp, .cc, .cxx, .hpp, .h, .hxx). Other languages are not parsed.

faq.question: How fast are MCP symbol searches?
faq.answer: MCP tools query a pre-built SQLite index, making them 10-100x faster than grep on large codebases.

faq.question: Does this work with multiple projects?
faq.answer: Yes. Each project has its own .claude/repo-map.db. MCP tools automatically adapt to your current directory.

faq.question: Is my code data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. All data stays local. No network calls are made. Index files are stored only in your project's .claude directory.

faq.question: Why is the first query slow?
faq.answer: On first use, the MCP server indexes your codebase in the background. Subsequent queries return instantly from the SQLite index.

faq.question: How is this different from Search/Grep tools?
faq.answer: Search/Grep scan file contents. MCP tools query a pre-indexed symbol database designed specifically for finding functions, classes, and methods by name.
